message:
feat(styles): add shimmer progress bar and extend badge tests

This commit adds an animated shimmer progress bar variant and expands
test coverage for the alert severity badge function.

ShimmerProgressBar Function (styles.go):
- New ShimmerProgressBar(percent, width, filled, empty, tick, colors) function
- Creates progress bar with animated shimmer effect on filled portion
- Parameters:
  - percent: Fill percentage (0.0-1.0, clamped)
  - width: Total character width
  - filled: Character for filled portion (e.g., "█")
  - empty: Character for empty portion (e.g., "░")
  - tick: Animation frame counter (0 = no shimmer)
  - colors: Gradient color stops (defaults to Blue→Green)
- Uses existing GradientText for base gradient
- Applies Shimmer() effect when tick > 0
- Empty portion uses Surface1 color from theme

Use Case:
The shimmer effect draws attention to critical metrics like
high context usage (>80%) where visual emphasis helps operators
quickly identify agents needing attention.

Badge Test Improvements (badges_test.go):
- Extend AlertSeverityBadge test cases with severity aliases:
  - "p0", "sev2" and other common severity notations
- Add severityLabel() helper function that maps:
  - critical/crit/p0/sev0 → "critical"
  - high/p1/sev1 → "high"
  - medium/med/p2/sev2 → "medium"
  - low/p3/sev3 → "low"
  - info → "info"
- Tests now verify badge contains normalized label

This ensures AlertSeverityBadge handles the various severity naming
conventions used across different systems (P0/P1, Sev0/Sev1, etc.).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
